def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    threshold = params['threshold']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    
    # Calculate support/resistance levels
    df['high_rolling'] = df['high'].rolling(window).max()
    df['low_rolling'] = df['low'].rolling(window).min()
    
    # Calculate ATR for volatility filter
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window).average_true_range()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price approaches support with low volatility
    long_condition = (df['close'] <= df['low_rolling'] * (1 + threshold)) & (df['close'] > df['low_rolling']) & (df['atr'] < df['atr'].rolling(atr_window).mean() * atr_multiplier)
    
    # Short when price approaches resistance with low volatility
    short_condition = (df['close'] >= df['high_rolling'] * (1 - threshold)) & (df['close'] < df['high_rolling']) & (df['atr'] < df['atr'].rolling(atr_window).mean() * atr_multiplier)
    
    signals[long_condition] = 1
    signals[short_condition] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/GBP exhibits mean-reverting behavior at key support/resistance levels, with reduced volatility confirming potential reversal zones.
